mnemonics: S.T.I.C.K. for Data Warehouse Features: Subject-Oriented, Time-Variant, Integrated, Consistent, Non-Volatile (Kept static)
keyPoints: Subject-Oriented: Data is organized around key business subjects like customers or products.
Integrated: Data is collected from multiple sources and integrated into a consistent format.
Time-Variant: Data is stored to provide a historical perspective, often with timestamps.
commonMistakes: Treating a data warehouse like a transactional database (OLTP), expecting real-time updates and high-frequency writes.
Loading data without proper cleansing and transformation, leading to poor data quality and inconsistent reporting.
Failing to properly model the data for analytical queries, resulting in slow report generation and poor performance.
